title: NIS2 Cyber Hygiene and Training Compliance

compliance.assurance_rationale: NIS2 Art.21(2)(g) — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NIS2 Art.21(2)(g) (Cyber-hygiene and training) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.2.10: NIS2 Cyber Hygiene and Training Compliance.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.2.10' running on sourcetype ms:o365:management and supporting catalogue-defined data sources, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

dataSources: `index=training` (LMS completion records via CSV/HEC), `index=email` `sourcetype="ms:o365:management"` (phishing simulation results), `` `notable` `` macro
app: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365 (Splunkbase 4055), CSV lookup (LMS export)
spl:
| inputlookup nis2_training_completion.csv
| eval days_since_training=round((now()-strptime(completion_date,"%Y-%m-%d"))/86400, 0)
| eval overdue=if(days_since_training > 365 OR isnull(completion_date), 1, 0)
| stats count as total_staff, sum(overdue) as overdue_count, avg(days_since_training) as avg_days_since
| eval compliance_pct=round(100*(total_staff-overdue_count)/total_staff, 1)
| table total_staff, overdue_count, compliance_pct, avg_days_since
description: Article 21(2)(g) requires basic cyber hygiene practices and cybersecurity training for all staff. This use case tracks training completion rates, identifies overdue personnel, and correlates training gaps with security incident involvement — proving that awareness is delivered, measured, and effective.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NIS2 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes email, training and sourcetypes ms:o365:management. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Export LMS completion data to `nis2_training_completion.csv` via scheduled script or HEC; (2) include all NIS2-scope employees (not just IT); (3) alert when compliance drops below 90%; (4) correlate training-overdue users with notable events to demonstrate risk-based prioritisation; (5) track phishing simulation click rates as effectiveness evidence. Indexes required: `index=email`, `index=training`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=ms:o365:management`.
visualization: Single value (compliance %), Bar chart (completion by department), Table (overdue staff), Line chart (compliance trend quarterly).
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.

This helps maintain steady compliance posture and reduces manual review effort.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365 (Splunkbase 4055), CSV lookup (LMS export).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=training` (LMS completion records via CSV/HEC), `index=email` `sourcetype="ms:o365:management"` (phishing simulation results), `` `notable` `` macro.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Export LMS completion data to `nis2_training_completion.csv` via scheduled script or HEC; (2) include all NIS2-scope employees (not just IT); (3) alert when compliance drops below 90%; (4) correlate training-overdue users with notable events to demonstrate risk-based prioritisation; (5) track phishing simulation click rates as effectiveness evidence.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
| inputlookup nis2_training_completion.csv
| eval days_since_training=round((now()-strptime(completion_date,"%Y-%m-%d"))/86400, 0)
| eval overdue=if(days_since_training > 365 OR isnull(completion_date), 1, 0)
| stats count as total_staff, sum(overdue) as overdue_count, avg(days_since_training) as avg_days_since
| eval compliance_pct=round(100*(total_staff-overdue_count)/total_staff, 1)
| table total_staff, overdue_count, compliance_pct, avg_days_since
```

Understanding this SPL

**NIS2 Cyber Hygiene and Training Compliance (Art. 21(2)(g))** — Article 21(2)(g) requires basic cyber hygiene practices and cybersecurity training for all staff. This use case tracks training completion rates, identifies overdue personnel, and correlates training gaps with security incident involvement — proving that awareness is delivered, measured, and effective.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=training` (LMS completion records via CSV/HEC), `index=email` `sourcetype="ms:o365:management"` (phishing simulation results), `` `notable` `` macro. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365 (Splunkbase 4055), CSV lookup (LMS export). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Loads rows via `inputlookup` (KV store or CSV lookup) for enrichment or reporting.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **days_since_training** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **overdue** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `stats` aggregates the pipeline (counts, distinct values, sums, percentiles, etc.) into fewer rows.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **compliance_pct** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.

Output fields: avg_days_since, compliance_pct, days_since_training, overdue, overdue_count, total_staff.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Single value (compliance %), Bar chart (completion by department), Table (overdue staff), Line chart (compliance trend quarterly).
